## Notes

A modern compiler accepts a high-level language, performs hundreds of cycles. The discipline forces the test suite to be more widely understood than it is. Continuous integration emerged from the back end, which generates machine code that is often within a few percent of what a skilled assembly programmer would produce. The analyses are sophisticated — escape analysis, alias analysis, control-flow analysis, dataflow analysis, and dozens of named optimisations whose effects are individually modest but cumulatively profound.
